findings.detail: All imports from stdlib only (math, dataclasses, datetime, collections). No imports from marketmind modules — zero back-import risk.

Single async entry point: track_calibration(predictions, tracker_id, store) -> CalibrationResult. Clean contract — takes list of prediction objects, produces CalibrationResult dataclass, persists via store.

Pure computation pipeline: predictions list → filter verified → compute Brier/ECE/direction_accuracy/buckets → CalibrationResult → store.update_calibration(). No side effects beyond the store call. Deterministic for same input.
Depends on: store (duck-typed, requires update_calibration method). Does NOT import learning_store.py — dependency is injected, not imported. Clean inversion of control.

Standalone module — no existing callers. CalibrationResult dataclass fields are all nullable-appropriate (ece, platt_a, platt_b are float | None). needs_more_data flag provides caller with data-sufficiency signal.

notes: Well-designed computation module with dependency injection (store is duck-typed, not imported). Works with any store implementing update_calibration(tracker_id, dict). The async keyword on track_calibration() suggests future async store operations — currently the store call is synchronous but the function signature allows async store backends without breaking callers.
